title: Lastrea stellatopilosa
taxonomy: Lastrea stellatopilosa is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is native to China and is a part of the Pteridophyta division of plants. The classification of Lastrea stellatopilosa has been subject to change over time, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Athyrium genus within the Lastrea subgenus. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the current understanding of plant systematics. Further research may be necessary to confirm the precise classification of Lastrea stellatopilosa.
uses: Lastrea stellatopilosa is a species of fern known for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is required to confirm its efficacy. The plant's uses are largely traditional and have been studied in Asian cultures. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. However, the specific medicinal uses of Lastrea stellatopilosa are not well-documented and require further investigation. Further research is needed to fully understand its potential applications in medicine.
